steps.title: Understand vertical shifts
steps.content: Shifting a graph DOWN by $k$ units subtracts $k$ from the output: $g(x) = f(x) - k$.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$g(x) = \frac{7}{2}x^3$': This scales the function vertically by $\frac{1}{2}$, not a vertical shift. ✗
  • Choice B — '$g(x) = 7x^{\frac{3}{2}}$': This changes the exponent, producing a completely different function. ✗
  • Choice C — '$g(x) = 7x^3 + 2$': Adding 2 shifts the graph UP 2 units, not down. ✗
  • Choice D — '$g(x) = 7x^3 - 2$': Subtracting 2 shifts the graph DOWN 2 units. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
